Container.Name: composer
Container.Overview: Lightweight, self-hosted Docker Compose management platform with GitOps, pipelines, and RBAC. Think Dockge simplicity meets Portainer power.[br][br]Works out of the box with embedded SQLite -- no external database needed. Optionally use PostgreSQL for multi-instance deployments.[br][br]1. Install this container[br]2. Open the web UI and create your admin account[br]3. Start managing your Docker Compose stacks[br][br]For PostgreSQL: set Database URL to your connection string. Leave it empty for SQLite (default).
Container.Requires: Docker socket access for container management. PostgreSQL is optional (SQLite is built-in).

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Composer web UI and API port.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Docker socket for container management. Required.

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Persistent data (SQLite database, encryption keys). Required.

Config.Name: Stacks Directory
Config.Description: Directory to store compose stack files.

Config.Name: Database URL
Config.Description: Leave empty for SQLite (recommended). Set to postgres://user:pass@host:5432/db for PostgreSQL.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file permissions (Unraid default: 99).

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file permissions (Unraid default: 100).

Config.Name: Valkey URL
Config.Description: Valkey/Redis URL for session caching (optional). Leave empty to disable.

Config.Name: Cookie Secure
Config.Description: Set to true if behind an HTTPS reverse proxy.
